question: What are the famous title of the artists associated with volumes with more than 2 weeks on top?
context: CREATE TABLE artist (Famous_Title VARCHAR, Artist_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE volume (Artist_ID VARCHAR, Weeks_on_Top INTEGER)
original_sql: SELECT T1.Famous_Title FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 2
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    database: your_database
    schema: your_schema
    tables:
      - name: artist
        identifier: artist
      - name: volume
        identifier: volume
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_artist
    description: Staging model for artist table
    columns:
      - name: famous_title
        description: The famous title of the artist
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: artist_id
        description: The ID of the artist

  - name: stg_volume
    description: Staging model for volume table
    columns:
      - name: artist_id
        description: The ID of the artist
      - name: weeks_on_top
        description: The number of weeks the artist was on top
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

SELECT
  Famous_Title AS famous_title,
  Artist_ID AS artist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_volume.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

SELECT
  Artist_ID AS artist_id,
  Weeks_on_Top AS weeks_on_top
FROM {{ source('raw', 'volume') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_artist_volume
    description: Report of artists with more than 2 weeks on top
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per Artist_ID
    columns:
      - name: famous_title
        description: The famous title of the artist
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: artist_id
        description: The ID of the artist
      - name: weeks_on_top
        description: The number of weeks the artist was on top
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_artist_volume.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH stg_artist AS (
  SELECT
    famous_title,
    artist_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }}
),

stg_volume AS (
  SELECT
    artist_id,
    weeks_on_top
  FROM {{ ref('stg_volume') }}
)

SELECT
  sa.famous_title,
  sa.artist_id,
  sv.weeks_on_top
FROM stg_artist sa
JOIN stg_volume sv ON sa.artist_id = sv.artist_id
WHERE sv.weeks_on_top > 2
</file>

original_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Hardware_Model_name FROM screen_mode AS T1 JOIN phone AS T2 ON T1.Graphics_mode = T2.screen_mode WHERE T1.Type = "Graphics" OR t2.Company_name = "Nokia Corporation"

      - name: phone

</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

models:
  - name: stg_screen_mode
    description: Staging model for screen_mode table

)

  type

<file path="models/staging/stg_phone.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ source('raw', 'phone') }}
)

  screen_mode

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_nokia_graphics_models
    description: Report of Nokia graphics models
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per Hardware_Model_name
    columns:
      - name: hardware_model_name
        description: Name of the hardware model
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_nokia_graphics_models.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH stg_screen_mode AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_screen_mode') }}
),

stg_phone AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_phone') }}
)

SELECT DISTINCT
  hardware_model_name
FROM stg_phone
WHERE screen_mode IN (
  SELECT graphics_mode
  FROM stg_screen_mode
  WHERE type = 'Graphics'
)
OR hardware_model_name = 'Nokia Corporation'
</file>

question: What are the ids and names of the web accelerators that are compatible with two or more browsers?

original_sql: SELECT T1.id, T1.Name FROM web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T2.accelerator_id = T1.id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2

<file path="models/staging/stg_accelerator_compatible_browser.sql">

)

  accelerator_id AS accelerator_id

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_accelerator_client_count
    description: "Fact table containing count of accelerators with compatible browsers"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per accelerator_id"
    columns:
      - name: id
        description: "Unique identifier for the web client accelerator"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: Name
        description: "Name of the web client accelerator"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: compatible_browser_count
        description: "Count of compatible browsers for each accelerator"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_accelerator_client_count.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH stg_web_client_accelerator AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_web_client_accelerator') }}
),

stg_accelerator_compatible_browser AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_accelerator_compatible_browser') }}
)

SELECT
  wca.id AS id,
  wca.Name AS Name,
  COUNT(acb.accelerator_id) AS compatible_browser_count
FROM stg_web_client_accelerator wca
JOIN stg_accelerator_compatible_browser acb ON acb.accelerator_id = wca.id
GROUP BY wca.id, wca.Name
HAVING COUNT(acb.accelerator_id) >= 2
</file>

question: find the program owners that have some programs in both morning and night time.
context: CREATE TABLE broadcast (program_id VARCHAR, Time_of_day VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE program (owner VARCHAR, program_id VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT t1.owner FROM program AS t1 JOIN broadcast AS t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id WHERE t2.Time_of_day = "Morning" INTERSECT SELECT t1.owner FROM program AS t1 JOIN broadcast AS t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id WHERE t2.Time_of_day = "Night"
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    database: null
    schema: null
    tables:
      - name: program
        description: "Raw program data"
      - name: broadcast
        description: "Raw broadcast data"
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_program
    description: "Staging model for program data"
    columns:
      - name: owner
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: program_id
  - name: stg_broadcast
    description: "Staging model for broadcast data"
    columns:
      - name: program_id
      - name: time_of_day
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_program.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ source('raw', 'program') }}
)

SELECT
  owner,
  program_id
FROM source_data
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_broadcast.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ source('raw', 'broadcast') }}
)

SELECT
  program_id,
  time_of_day
FROM source_data
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_program_owner_analysis
    description: "Report showing program owners who broadcast in both Morning and Night"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per program owner
    columns:
      - name: owner
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_program_owner_analysis.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH
stg_program AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_program') }}
),
stg_broadcast AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_broadcast') }}
),
morning_owners AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT t1.owner
  FROM stg_program t1
  JOIN stg_broadcast t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id
  WHERE t2.time_of_day = 'Morning'
),
night_owners AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT t1.owner
  FROM stg_program t1
  JOIN stg_broadcast t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id
  WHERE t2.time_of_day = 'Night'
)

SELECT DISTINCT owner
FROM morning_owners
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT owner
FROM night_owners
</file>

</file>

  AND sportsinfo_cte.onscholarship = 'Y'

question: What is the code of airport that has fewest number of flights?

original_sql: SELECT T1.AirportCode FROM AIRPORTS AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.AirportCode = T2.DestAirport OR T1.AirportCode = T2.SourceAirport GROUP BY T1.AirportCode ORDER BY COUNT(*) LIMIT 1

    materialized='view'
  )

  lower(source_airport) as source_airport

<file path="models/marts/core/dim_airport.sql">

  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH
stg_airports AS (
  SELECT
    airport_code
  FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }}
),
stg_flights AS (
  SELECT
    dest_airport,
    source_airport
  FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
)

SELECT
  T1.airport_code
FROM stg_airports AS T1
JOIN stg_flights AS T2 ON T1.airport_code = T2.dest_airport OR T1.airport_code = T2.source_airport
GROUP BY T1.airport_code
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_intersected_highschooler_names
    description: Report showing highschooler names who are both friends and liked by others.
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per unique highschooler name
    columns:
      - name: name
        description: Name of the student who is both a friend and liked by others.
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_intersected_highschooler_names.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH stg_friend AS (
  SELECT student_id, liked_id FROM {{ ref('stg_friend') }}
),

stg_highschooler AS (
  SELECT name, id FROM {{ ref('stg_highschooler') }}
),

stg_likes AS (
  SELECT student_id, liked_id FROM {{ ref('stg_likes') }}
),

friend_with_highschooler AS (
  SELECT
    h.name AS name,
    f.student_id AS student_id,
    f.liked_id AS liked_id
  FROM stg_friend f
  JOIN stg_highschooler h ON f.student_id = h.id
),

likes_with_highschooler AS (
  SELECT
    h.name AS name,
    l.student_id AS student_id,
    l.liked_id AS liked_id
  FROM stg_likes l
  JOIN stg_highschooler h ON l.liked_id = h.id
)

SELECT DISTINCT name
FROM (
  SELECT name FROM friend_with_highschooler
  INTERSECT
  SELECT name FROM likes_with_highschooler
)
</file>

original_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year < 2009 INTERSECT SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year > 2011

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_visitor_museum_intersection.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH
stg_visitor AS (
  SELECT name, id FROM {{ ref('stg_visitor') }}
),
stg_visit AS (
  SELECT visitor_id, Museum_ID FROM {{ ref('stg_visit') }}
),
stg_museum AS (

)

SELECT

FROM stg_visitor v
JOIN stg_visit vi ON v.id = vi.visitor_id

FROM stg_visitor v

      model_type: "report"
      grain: "1 row per campus"

      - name: campus
        description: "Campus name"
        tests:
          - not_null

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_campus_faculty_2002.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH
stg_campuses AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_campuses') }}
),
stg_faculty AS (

  FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }}
)

                   JOIN stg_faculty AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.campus

original_sql: SELECT T3.Shop_Name, T2.Carrier FROM stock AS T1 JOIN device AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T1.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    database: {{ env_var('DBT_ENV_VAR_DB_NAME') }}
    schema: {{ env_var('DBT_ENV_VAR_DB_SCHEMA') }}
    tables:
      - name: stock
        description: "Raw data for stock table"
      - name: device
        description: "Raw data for device table"
      - name: shop
        description: "Raw data for shop table"
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_stock
    description: "Staging model for stock table"
    columns:
      - name: device_id
        description: "ID of the device"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: shop_id
        description: "ID of the shop"
        tests:
          - not_null
  - name: stg_device
    description: "Staging model for device table"
    columns:
      - name: carrier
        description: "Carrier of the device"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: device_id
        description: "ID of the device"
        tests:
          - not_null
  - name: stg_shop
    description: "Staging model for shop table"
    columns:
      - name: shop_name
        description: "Name of the shop"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: shop_id
        description: "ID of the shop"
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_stock.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view'
  )
}}

SELECT
  LOWER(device_id) AS device_id,
  LOWER(shop_id) AS shop_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'stock') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_device.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view'
  )
}}

SELECT
  LOWER(carrier) AS carrier,
  LOWER(device_id) AS device_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'device') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_shop.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view'
  )
}}

SELECT
  LOWER(shop_name) AS shop_name,
  LOWER(shop_id) AS shop_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'shop') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_shop_device_carrier
    description: "Report showing shop names and their associated device carriers"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per Device_ID, Shop_ID
    columns:
      - name: shop_name
        description: "Name of the shop"
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: carrier
        description: "Carrier of the device"
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_shop_device_carrier.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH stg_stock AS (
  SELECT
    device_id,
    shop_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_stock') }}
),

stg_device AS (
  SELECT
    carrier,
    device_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_device') }}
),

stg_shop AS (
  SELECT
    shop_name,
    shop_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_shop') }}
)

SELECT
  s.shop_name,
  d.carrier
FROM stg_stock ss
JOIN stg_device sd ON ss.device_id = sd.device_id
JOIN stg_shop s ON ss.shop_id = s.shop_id
</file>

question: What are the names of the tourist attractions that have parking or shopping as their feature details?
context: CREATE TABLE Tourist_Attraction_Features (tourist_attraction_id VARCHAR, Feature_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Features (Feature_ID VARCHAR, feature_Details VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Tourist_Attractions (Name VARCHAR, tourist_attraction_id VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 ON T1.tourist_attraction_id = T2.tourist_attraction_id JOIN Features AS T3 ON T2.Feature_ID = T3.Feature_ID WHERE T3.feature_Details = 'park' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 ON T1.tourist_attraction_id = T2.tourist_attraction_id JOIN Features AS T3 ON T2.Feature_ID = T3.Feature_ID WHERE T3.feature_Details = 'shopping'
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    database: your_database
    schema: public
    tables:
      - name: Tourist_Attractions
        description: Raw table containing tourist attraction names and IDs.
      - name: Tourist_Attraction_Features
        description: Raw table mapping tourist attractions to their features.
      - name: Features
        description: Raw table containing feature details for tourist attractions.
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_Tourist_Attractions
    description: Staging model for tourist attractions.
    columns:
      - name: tourist_attraction_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_Tourist_Attraction_Features
    description: Staging model for tourist attraction features.
    columns:
      - name: tourist_attraction_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: feature_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_Features
    description: Staging model for features.
    columns:
      - name: feature_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: feature_details
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_Tourist_Attractions.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view',
    schema='staging'
  )
}}

SELECT
  tourist_attraction_id,
  name AS tourist_attraction_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Tourist_Attractions') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_Tourist_Attraction_Features.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view',
    schema='staging'
  )
}}

SELECT
  tourist_attraction_id,
  Feature_ID AS feature_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Tourist_Attraction_Features') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_Features.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view',
    schema='staging'
  )
}}

SELECT
  Feature_ID AS feature_id,
  feature_Details AS feature_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Features') }}
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_park_shopping_attractions.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='view',
    schema='intermediate'
  )
}}

WITH
  park_attractions AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT T1.tourist_attraction_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_Tourist_Attractions') }} T1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_Tourist_Attraction_Features') }} T2
      ON T1.tourist_attraction_id = T2.tourist_attraction_id
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_Features') }} T3
      ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id
    WHERE T3.feature_details = 'park'
  ),
  shopping_attractions AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT T1.tourist_attraction_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_Tourist_Attractions') }} T1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_Tourist_Attraction_Features') }} T2
      ON T1.tourist_attraction_id = T2.tourist_attraction_id
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_Features') }} T3
      ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id
    WHERE T3.feature_details = 'shopping'
  )

SELECT DISTINCT tourist_attraction_id
FROM park_attractions
UNION
SELECT DISTINCT tourist_attraction_id
FROM shopping_attractions
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_park_shopping_attractions
    description: Report of tourist attractions that are either parks or shopping areas.
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per tourist attraction name
    columns:
      - name: tourist_attraction_name
        description: Name of the tourist attraction.
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_park_shopping_attractions.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table',
    schema='marts'
  )
}}

WITH
  stg_tourist_attractions AS (
    SELECT
      tourist_attraction_id,
      tourist_attraction_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_Tourist_Attractions') }}
  ),
  int_park_shopping_attractions AS (
    SELECT
      tourist_attraction_id
    FROM {{ ref('int_park_shopping_attractions') }}
  )

SELECT DISTINCT
  stg_tourist_attractions.tourist_attraction_name
FROM int_park_shopping_attractions
JOIN stg_tourist_attractions
  ON int_park_shopping_attractions.tourist_attraction_id = stg_tourist_attractions.tourist_attraction_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_address_rental_metrics
    description: Fact table containing address rental metrics
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: 1 row per address_id
    columns:
      - name: line_1
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: address_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: monthly_rental_avg
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_address_rental_metrics.sql">
{{
  config(materialized='table')
}}

WITH
stg_Addresses AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_Addresses') }}
),
stg_Student_Addresses AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_Student_Addresses') }}
)

SELECT
  stg_Addresses.line_1,
  stg_Student_Addresses.address_id,
  AVG(stg_Student_Addresses.monthly_rental) AS monthly_rental_avg
FROM stg_Addresses
JOIN stg_Student_Addresses ON stg_Addresses.address_id = stg_Student_Addresses.address_id
GROUP BY stg_Student_Addresses.address_id, stg_Addresses.line_1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

  - name: fct_lead_vocal_performance
    description: Fact table for lead vocal performances
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: 1 row per vocals record
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: ["not_null"]
      - name: songid
        tests: ["not_null"]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_lead_vocal_performance.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH
stg_vocals AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_vocals') }}
),
stg_songs AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_songs') }}
),
joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.title,
    s.songid
  FROM stg_songs s
  JOIN stg_vocals v ON s.songid = v.songid
)

SELECT DISTINCT
  title,
  songid
FROM joined_data
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM stg_songs s2
  JOIN stg_vocals v2 ON s2.songid = v2.songid
  WHERE s2.title = joined_data.title
    AND v2.type = 'lead'
)
</file>

original_sql: SELECT t3.characteristic_name FROM products AS t1 JOIN product_characteristics AS t2 ON t1.product_id = t2.product_id JOIN CHARACTERISTICS AS t3 ON t2.characteristic_id = t3.characteristic_id GROUP BY t3.characteristic_name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2

version: 2

    description: Dimension table with characteristic counts

      grain: 1 row per characteristic_name
    columns:
      - name: characteristic_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/marts/core/dim_characteristic_counts.sql">
{{
  config(materialized='table')
}}

WITH stg_products_cte AS (
  SELECT
    product_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}
),
stg_product_characteristics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    product_id,
    characteristic_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_product_characteristics') }}
),
stg_CHARACTERISTICS_cte AS (
  SELECT
    characteristic_name,
    characteristic_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_CHARACTERISTICS') }}
),
joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    spc.product_id,
    sc.characteristic_name
  FROM stg_product_characteristics_cte spc
  JOIN stg_CHARACTERISTICS_cte sc ON spc.characteristic_id = sc.characteristic_id
  JOIN stg_products_cte sp ON spc.product_id = sp.product_id
)
SELECT
  characteristic_name
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY characteristic_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    surname AS surname,

<file path="models/staging/stg_pitstops.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

SELECT
    driverid AS driverid

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_driver_analysis
    description: Report showing drivers with specific pitstop and results counts
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per driverid
    columns:
      - name: surname
        data_type: VARCHAR
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: driverid
        data_type: VARCHAR
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_driver_analysis.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH
stg_drivers AS (
    SELECT
        surname,
        driverid
    FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }}
),

stg_pitstops AS (
    SELECT
        driverid
    FROM {{ ref('stg_pitstops') }}
),

stg_results AS (
    SELECT
        driverid
    FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}
),

drivers_with_pitstops AS (
    SELECT
        d.surname,
        d.driverid
    FROM stg_drivers d
    JOIN stg_pitstops p ON d.driverid = p.driverid
    GROUP BY d.surname, d.driverid
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 11
),

drivers_with_results AS (
    SELECT
        d.surname,
        d.driverid
    FROM stg_drivers d
    JOIN stg_results r ON d.driverid = r.driverid
    GROUP BY d.surname, d.driverid
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
)

SELECT
    surname,
    driverid
FROM drivers_with_pitstops
INTERSECT
SELECT
    surname,
    driverid
FROM drivers_with_results
</file>

original_sql: SELECT t1.id, t1.maker FROM car_makers AS t1 JOIN model_list AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.maker GROUP BY t1.id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 INTERSECT SELECT t1.id, t1.maker FROM car_makers AS t1 JOIN model_list AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.maker JOIN car_names AS t3 ON t2.model = t3.model GROUP BY t1.id HAVING COUNT(*) > 3

version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_car_makers_with_models
    description: Report of car makers with models.
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: 1 row per car_makers.id
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: maker
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_car_makers_with_models.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table',
    schema='marts'
  )
}}

WITH
stg_car_makers AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_car_makers') }}
),
stg_model_list AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_model_list') }}
),
joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    stg_car_makers.id,
    stg_car_makers.maker,
    COUNT(*) AS model_count
  FROM stg_car_makers
  JOIN stg_model_list ON stg_car_makers.id = stg_model_list.maker
  GROUP BY stg_car_makers.id, stg_car_makers.maker
),
filtered_data AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    maker
  FROM joined_data
  WHERE model_count >= 2
),
final_data AS (
  SELECT
    stg_car_makers.id,
    stg_car_makers.maker,
    COUNT(*) AS car_name_count
  FROM filtered_data
  JOIN stg_model_list ON filtered_data.id = stg_model_list.maker
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_car_names') }} ON stg_model_list.model = {{ ref('stg_car_names') }}.model
  GROUP BY stg_car_makers.id, stg_car_makers.maker
)

SELECT
  id,
  maker
FROM final_data
WHERE car_name_count > 3
</file>

  SUM(order_quantity)::INTEGER AS order_quantity_sum
FROM joined_data
WHERE customer_name = 'Rodrick Heaney'
GROUP BY customer_id
</file>

    description: Report of customers who ordered both Latte and Americano
    meta:
      model_type: report

    columns:

        tests:

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_latte_americano_customers.sql">
{{
  config(
    materialized='table'
  )
}}

WITH stg_customers AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),
stg_customer_orders AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_customer_orders') }}
),
stg_order_items AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }}
),
stg_products AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}
),

latte_customers AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_name
  FROM stg_customers c
  JOIN stg_customer_orders co ON c.customer_id = co.customer_id
  JOIN stg_order_items oi ON co.order_id = oi.order_id
  JOIN stg_products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE p.product_details = 'Latte'
),

americano_customers AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_name
  FROM stg_customers c
  JOIN stg_customer_orders co ON c.customer_id = co.customer_id
  JOIN stg_order_items oi ON co.order_id = oi.order_id
  JOIN stg_products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE p.product_details = 'Americano'
)

SELECT
  customer_name
FROM latte_customers
INTERSECT
SELECT
  customer_name
FROM americano_customers
</file>
